name: Thai Beef Stir-Fry
intro: A unique peanut sauce complements the colorful combination of tender sirloin, cauliflower, carrots, broccoli and mushrooms. I like to serve it over spaghetti, but fried noodles work well too.
method: In a small bowl, combine the brown sugar, cornstarch, beef broth, soy sauce, onion powder, garlic powder, ground ginger, and hot pepper sauce until smooth.
Heat 3 tablespoons of oil in a large cast iron skillet or wok, then sauté the beef until the meat is no longer pink. Transfer the beef to a plate.
In the same wok, heat the remaining oil, then sauté the broccoli, cauliflowers, and carrots for 5 minutes.
Add the soup mixture (Step 1) to the wok. Let it boil. Cook, stirring, for about 2 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium, then add ground beef (Step 2) and peanut butter. Cook, stirring, until the peanut butter is incorporated.
Serve with pasta and top with peanuts before eating.
